content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 18 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic)
**Hebrew Base:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), LC PDF pages 215–216 | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus, pa_0154–pa_0155
**Verse count:** 22

---

**1** The Levitical priests—all the tribe of Levi—shall have no portion or inheritance with Israel; the fire offerings of the LORD and his inheritance they shall eat.

**2** And they shall have no inheritance among their brothers; the LORD is their inheritance, as he promised them.

**3** And this shall be the priests' due [mishpat ha-kohanim] from the people, from those who offer a sacrifice—whether ox or sheep: they shall give to the priest the shoulder and the two cheeks and the stomach.

**4** The firstfruits of your grain, your new wine, and your fresh oil, and the first fleece of your flock, you shall give to him.

**5** For the LORD your God has chosen him out of all your tribes to stand and minister in the name of the LORD—him and his sons, all his days.

**6** And if a Levite comes from any of your towns out of all Israel, where he sojourns, and comes with all the desire of his soul to the place which the LORD will choose—

**7** then he shall minister in the name of the LORD his God, like all his fellow Levites who stand to minister there before the LORD.

**8** They shall have equal portions to eat, besides what he receives from the sale of his patrimony.

**9** When you come into the land which the LORD your God is giving you, you shall not learn to do according to the abominable practices of those nations.

**10** There shall not be found among you anyone who makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire, or who practices divination, or tells fortunes, or interprets omens, or is a sorcerer,

**11** or who casts a spell, or who consults a medium or a spiritist, or who inquires of the dead.

**12** For everyone who does these things is an abomination to the LORD, and because of these abominations the LORD your God is driving them out before you.

**13** You shall be wholehearted [tamim] before the LORD your God.

**14** For these nations which you are about to dispossess listen to fortune-tellers and to diviners, but as for you—the LORD your God has not permitted you to do this.

**15** A prophet like me the LORD your God will raise up for you from your midst, from among your brothers—you shall listen to him—

**16** just as you desired of the LORD your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly, when you said, "Let me not again hear the voice of the LORD my God or see this great fire again, that I may not die."

**17** And the LORD said to me: "They have done well in what they have spoken.

**18** A prophet I will raise up for them from the midst of their brothers, like you; and I will put my words in his mouth, and he shall speak to them all that I command him.

**19** And it shall be that whoever does not listen to my words which he speaks in my name—I myself will require it of him.

**20** But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in my name which I have not commanded him to speak, or who speaks in the name of other gods—that prophet shall die.

**21** And if you say in your heart, 'How shall we know the word which the LORD has not spoken?'—

**22** when a prophet speaks in the name of the LORD, if the word does not come about and does not come true, that is the word which the LORD has not spoken; the prophet has spoken it presumptuously. You need not be afraid of him.

---

## SOPHIA's Council Notes

**v.1 — "portion or inheritance":** All three agents agree on this rendering of *cheleq ve-nachalah*. SOPHIA adopts the standard rendering.

**v.2 — "as he promised them" vs. "as he spoke to him":** EMET reads "as he spoke to him" (singular); LOGOS "as he promised them" (plural); PNEUMA "just as he promised them." The MT has singular *lo* ("to him") referring to the tribe collectively. SOPHIA follows EMET's singular but renders "promised" rather than "spoke to" for natural English. See disagreements.

**v.3 — mishpat ha-kohanim:** SOPHIA preserves the Hebrew term in brackets per project convention. This is a legal term for the priest's customary entitlement.

**v.5 — "all his days" vs. "forever":** EMET reads "him and his sons, forever" (*le'olam*); LOGOS "him and his sons, all his days." SOPHIA follows LOGOS's "all his days" as more contextually appropriate — *le'olam* in this context means "in perpetuity" (the ongoing office), rendered naturally in English as "all his days."

**v.10 — "makes his son or his daughter pass through the fire":** All three agents preserve this rendering. LOGOS adds "as an offering" (interpretive); EMET and PNEUMA do not add it. SOPHIA follows EMET's literal rendering as the safest. See disagreements.

**v.13 — tamim:** SOPHIA adopts EMET's "wholehearted" for *tamim* here, which is the same word used for sacrificial animals' unblemished condition. LOGOS uses "blameless"; PNEUMA "blameless." "Wholehearted" better captures the relational completeness of the command in this context vs. the legal negativity of "blameless." See disagreements.

**v.15 — the prophet-like-Moses:** SOPHIA preserves the word order of the Hebrew more closely: "A prophet like me the LORD your God will raise up for you." The fronted "prophet like me" matches the Hebrew emphasis. All three agents agree on the substance. "From your midst, from among your brothers" preserves the doublet of the MT.

**vv.10–11 — the forbidden practices list:** SOPHIA follows the MT order closely (EMET most literal). LOGOS's rendering of the eight practices is clearest; SOPHIA adopts LOGOS for practices 1–4 and 6–8, and EMET for the fifth (*chover chaver* = "casts a spell").

**v.22 — "presumptuously" vs. "in arrogance":** EMET and LOGOS use "presumptuously" for *zadon*. PNEUMA the same. All agree.

*— SOPHIA*
